text: When you sign up somewhere new, what do you do for the password?

options.text: Same one I use everywhere. It's a strong one.
Tweak my usual one — add a number or change a letter
Make something different for important accounts
Let my password manager generate a random one

text: Do you use two-factor authentication — that extra code when you log in?

options.text: No, my password is enough
Only when a site forces me to
Yes — I get a text code on email and banking
Yes — I use an authenticator app or passkey

text: You get a text saying your bank account is locked — tap here to fix it. What do you do?

options.text: Tap the link. It looks legitimate.
Tap it, but check the page before typing anything
Search for the bank's real website instead
Open the bank's official app and ignore the text

text: A new app asks for your location, contacts, and microphone. What do you do?

options.text: Allow everything so it actually works
Allow most things — I don't really read the prompts
Only allow what clearly makes sense for the app
Deny by default and review app permissions regularly

text: Your phone has been asking you to update for two weeks. What happens?

options.text: I dismiss it. Updates always break something.
I'll get to it eventually. Probably.
I install it within a few days when I remember
Auto-updates are on — it just sorts itself out

text: You need to check your bank balance at a café. Free Wi-Fi is right there. What do you do?

options.text: Connect and log in to my bank — easy
Connect, but try to avoid banking on it
Only use it for non-sensitive stuff
Switch to mobile data for anything with a password

text: What kind of stuff do you typically share on social media?

options.text: Everything — location, school, daily plans, the lot
Quite a bit, but not my home address
General stuff only — no exact locations or schedules
Minimal, and my profile is set to friends only

text: You just bought something from a small online shop. What did you do with your card?

options.text: Saved it on the site — handy for next time
I save cards on big sites but not this one
Typed it in manually and didn't save
Used Apple Pay or Google Pay — no card number shared

text: You land on a page asking for your email and password. How do you decide if it's real?

options.text: If it looks right, I just log in
I check if the logo and design look familiar
I check the URL carefully before typing anything
I close it and go to the site directly myself

text: What happens to your old accounts from apps and sites you no longer use?

options.text: They're still out there. I never think about it.
I've deleted a couple, but most just sit there
I delete accounts I haven't touched in a while
I audit old accounts and use a spare email for sign-ups
